physics_facts: Moving at the speed of light isn't possible unless you are made of light.
The universe is expanding faster over time, driven by a force called dark energy.
Most of ordinary matter is empty space. If removed, the human race could fit in a sugar cube.
Most of the hydrogen atoms in our bodies were formed in the Big Bang 13.7 billion years ago.
Physics comes from the Greek word meaning 'science of nature.'
We are free to move in space, but stuck in one direction of time: the 'Arrow of Time.'
An estimated 100 trillion neutrinos pass through our bodies every second.
Heat naturally flows from warmer to cooler areas, according to the 2nd Law of Thermodynamics.
Everything that exists shows both wave-like and particle-like properties.
Nuclear physics is specifically the study of the nucleus of an atom.
Isaac Newton founded classical physics; Albert Einstein is the father of modern physics.
The Theory of One Electron states everything in the universe is the same electron.
Scientists have created crystals that can store oxygen, potentially replacing SCUBA tanks.
The human brain can create theoretical structures in up to 11 dimensions.
Life experiences can leave chemical markers on your DNA passed to future generations.
The world has three dimensions of space (length, width, depth) and one of time.
Gravity is actually the curvature of space and time.
If you could travel at the speed of light, time would effectively stop.
To reach the speed of light, a human body would require an infinite amount of energy.
The Sun and Moon look same-sized because the Moon is 400x smaller but 400x closer.
There are 4 fundamental forces: Strong Nuclear, Weak Nuclear, Electromagnetic, and Gravity.
A spoonful of matter from a neutron star would weigh more than Mt. Everest.
Atoms never truly 'touch' in the everyday sense because they are fuzzy probability fields.
Aerogel is 99.8% air and is often referred to as 'frozen smoke.'
The speed of darkness is exactly the same as the speed of light.
There are 118 elements on the periodic table, but none of them contain the letters J or Q.
About 80% of the universe is dark matter, which is completely invisible to our technology.
It takes a photon 170,000 years to leave the Sun's core, but only 8 minutes to reach Earth.
